description: Evaluation, judgment, preference between options, finding things better or worse
judge_target: evaluation, preference, judging one option as better than another
positive_prompts: The second draft is sharper than the first.
I prefer the rewrite for its restraint.
Her solution is more elegant than the brute-force approach.
His phrasing is clearer than the original.
I find the first proof more illuminating, even though both are valid.
The simpler design is better here, despite losing one feature.
She handled the criticism more gracefully than I would have.
The author's earlier essays read as fresher than the recent collection.
This version of the argument is stronger than the one in the appendix.
I'd rather have a partial result that's correct than a complete one that's shaky.
Her example is more vivid than the standard textbook one.
The minimalist staging suits this play better than a more ornate production would.
I prefer the chapter that hesitates over the one that asserts.
Her draft is more disciplined; his is more inventive — I'd take her draft for this audience.
The unhurried scene is the most affecting one in the film.
I find his polemic less persuasive than her measured analysis.
The shorter title is better than the original.
The painting in the smaller room is the strongest in the show.
I prefer the older translation; the new one is fluent but flat.
The plain prose carries more weight than the ornamented prose here.
Her counterargument is sharper than her positive case.
I'd rather have the reviewer's tough first draft than the polite revision.
The first half of the book is far better than the second.
Her early work is more original; her later work is more accomplished — I prefer the early.
The straightforward implementation is preferable to the clever one in this codebase.
I find the dissenting opinion more carefully reasoned than the majority opinion.
Her diagnosis is the better one because it accounts for the contradictory observation.
The corner office is no better than the one beside the window.
His lecture style is more engaging than his writing.
I prefer the question to the answer.
The second piano sonata is the strongest of the set.
The rough draft has more life than the polished version.
I'd take a serious objection over a glib agreement.
Her approach is sound; his is brilliant; for this purpose I'll take sound.
The smaller print run looks better on the page.
I prefer the unfinished sketch to the completed painting.
Her instinct on the personnel decision was better than the committee's recommendation.
The first session of the conference was the most rewarding.
I find the recursive proof more elegant than the constructive one.
The candidate's prior published work is stronger than her interview performance.
I'd prefer a clear no to a hedged yes.
The introductory chapter is excellent; the conclusion is merely adequate.
Her revisions made the piece tighter and more honest.
I prefer his first answer to his more polished second one.
The shorter ending is better than the original; the original was overstuffed.
Her judgment about the priority of the fixes was better than mine.
The cheap restaurant on the corner serves better food than the expensive one downtown.
I find the smaller venue more conducive to the music than the concert hall.
Her cover letter is more persuasive than her résumé.
I prefer the sketchbook to the finished portfolio.
